user_message: Just came out of consumption trend with Lucerne Publishing. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-03-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:06:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:09:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:13:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:15:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:18:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:22:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-J trap (Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-J failure mode (Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-J -- Proposes architecture pattern customer explicitly rejected in transcript
